description: Hand-labeled reference set for validating the Phase-7 outcome-class classifier (classify_outcomes.py). Each item is one Consequence node (by gkey) with its gold outcome_class. Stratified across the taxonomy with extra weight on the load-bearing severity-5-vs-4 boundary (single-train-vs-both) and the 'X inoperable / TS entered' boundary.
labeler: assistant (Claude Opus 4.8), deliberate per-item reading of each consequence + its LER context. This is a DIFFERENT process (different model, per-item reasoning) from the Sonnet batch it validates, but it is NOT independent human-expert ground truth. Treat labels as editable — correct any you disagree with and re-run --validate.
rubric_note: Discriminator for the messy cases: a FUNCTIONAL inoperability/failure of a safety component -> severity 4 (single) or 5 (both/all trains or explicit loss of function). An as-found VALUE/LIMIT/setpoint/leakage/calibration or administrative exceedance, component still functioning -> ts-violation-only (2). Most-severe-wins when a consequence names several effects. Reactor trip/scram is always reactor-trip-or-scram (4). 'Secondary containment inoperable' is treated as a functional barrier inoperability (4).
excluded_note: 220-2021-002-00 (Both Emergency Condensers) is deliberately absent: it is a few-shot example in prompts/outcome_classification.md, so scoring it would measure memorization, not generalization.
